implementation_notes: Implementation via task-work delegation. Files planned: 0, Files actual: 0
requirements_addressed: AC-006: real BuildQueuedPayload construction with provenance + timestamps
AC-008: single logger.info call with LOG_PREFIX_QUEUE_BUILD-prefixed message
AC-011: pydantic ValidationError caught at tool boundary
AC-013: seam test — round-trip through BuildQueuedPayload.model_validate_json
requirements_remaining: AC-lint: project-configured lint/format checks (black/ruff) not invoked here — code follows PEP 8 / 88-char line limit / type hints per .claude/rules/code-style.md, but the operator's CI tooling will run the formal lint step.

completion_promises.criterion_text: src/jarvis/tools/dispatch.py exposes queue_build(feature_id: str, feature_yaml_path: str, repo: str, branch: str = "main", originating_adapter: str = "terminal", correlation_id: str | None = None, parent_request_id: str | None = None) -> str decorated with @tool(parse_docstring=True).
completion_promises.evidence: Defined `queue_build` in src/jarvis/tools/dispatch.py with the exact signature and `@tool(parse_docstring=True)` decorator. Test class TestAC001SignatureAndDecorator (5 cases) verifies BaseTool instance, name, parameter list/order, default values, and `str` return annotation via get_type_hints.

Pasted the docstring verbatim from docs/design/FEAT-JARVIS-002/contracts/API-tools.md §3.2 into queue_build. Test class TestAC002Docstring verifies presence of contractual phrases ('Pattern A', 'ADR-SP-014', 'Phase 2', 'stub', Args section with all 7 named parameters, all four ERROR strings).

completion_promises.criterion_text: Validates feature_id against ^FEAT-[A-Z0-9]{3,12}$; rejects invalid with `ERROR: invalid_feature_id — must match FEAT-XXX pattern, got <value>`.
completion_promises.evidence: Compiled module-private `_FEATURE_ID_PATTERN = re.compile(r'^FEAT-[A-Z0-9]{3,12}$')`; rejected inputs return the documented string. TestAC003FeatureIdValidation parametrises 7 invalid values and asserts the prefix, 'must match FEAT-XXX pattern', and 'got <value>' substrings, plus a positive case.

completion_promises.criterion_text: Validates repo against ^[A-Za-z0-9._-]+/[A-Za-z0-9._-]+$; rejects invalid with `ERROR: invalid_repo — must be org/name format, got <value>`.
completion_promises.evidence: `_REPO_PATTERN` compiled with the documented regex; the boundary rejects with the exact ERROR string. TestAC004RepoValidation parametrises 6 invalid values + 1 valid value.

completion_promises.criterion_text: Validates originating_adapter in {terminal, telegram, dashboard, voice-reachy, slack, cli-wrapper}; rejects other with `ERROR: invalid_adapter — <value> not in allowed list`.
completion_promises.evidence: `_ALLOWED_ADAPTERS` is a frozenset of the six values; rejects with the documented string. TestAC005AdapterValidation parametrises 5 invalid values and all 6 allowed values.

completion_promises.criterion_text: Constructs real BuildQueuedPayload with triggered_by="jarvis", originating_adapter=<value>, correlation_id=correlation_id or new_correlation_id(), requested_at=now_utc(), queued_at=now_utc().
completion_promises.evidence: Inside queue_build the BuildQueuedPayload is instantiated with the listed fields, using `_now_utc()` (a thin wrapper over `datetime.now(timezone.utc)`) and `new_correlation_id()` from jarvis.tools._correlation when no caller-supplied id is given. TestAC006Ac007PayloadAndEnvelope round-trips the payload through model_validate_json and verifies the auto-generated correlation_id matches the UUID4 format.

completion_promises.criterion_text: Constructs real MessageEnvelope(source_id="jarvis", event_type=EventType.BUILD_QUEUED, correlation_id=..., payload=...).
completion_promises.evidence: MessageEnvelope is built with source_id='jarvis', event_type=EventType.BUILD_QUEUED, correlation_id=resolved id, payload=BuildQueuedPayload.model_dump(mode='json'). The seam test (TestAC013SeamContract.test_envelope_round_trips_with_payload_dict) validates JSON round-trip with the BUILD_QUEUED event type.

completion_promises.criterion_text: Emits exactly one logger.info call with message starting with LOG_PREFIX_QUEUE_BUILD and containing feature_id=<x> repo=<y> correlation_id=<z> topic=pipeline.build-queued.<x> payload_bytes=<n>.
completion_promises.evidence: logger.info uses `%s feature_id=%s repo=%s correlation_id=%s topic=%s payload_bytes=%d` with LOG_PREFIX_QUEUE_BUILD as the first arg. TestAC008LoggerInfo asserts exactly 1 record on logger 'jarvis.tools.dispatch', the message starts with LOG_PREFIX_QUEUE_BUILD, and contains all 5 documented key=value substrings.

Tool returns `json.dumps({"feature_id":..., "correlation_id":..., "queued_at": queued_at.isoformat(), "publish_target": subject, "status":"queued"})`. TestAC009QueueBuildAck verifies key set equality, exact field values, and ISO-8601 parseable queued_at.

subject = Topics.Pipeline.BUILD_QUEUED.format(feature_id=feature_id) is used both in the logger.info topic= field and as the ack['publish_target']. TestAC010Topic asserts ack['publish_target'] == Topics.Pipeline.BUILD_QUEUED.format(feature_id=feature_id) and that the literal expansion is 'pipeline.build-queued.FEAT-J002'.

completion_promises.criterion_text: Pydantic ValidationError caught at tool boundary → `ERROR: validation — <pydantic detail>` (ADR-ARCH-021).
completion_promises.evidence: BuildQueuedPayload + MessageEnvelope construction wrapped in `try: ... except ValidationError as exc: return f'ERROR: validation — {exc.errors()[0].get("msg", str(exc))}'` with a fallback (TypeError, ValueError) handler for non-string boundary inputs. TestAC011ValidationErrorBoundary passes feature_yaml_path=12345 and asserts the result starts with 'ERROR: validation'.

All early-validation paths return error strings; pydantic and TypeError/ValueError are caught around the constructor block; the rest of the function uses pure data ops (json.dumps, format). TestAC012NeverRaises parametrises four input shapes (each invalid in a different way) and asserts each returns a string without raising.

completion_promises.criterion_text: Seam test: dispatches succeed end-to-end through assemble_tool_list-wired supervisor fixture; payload JSON round-trips through BuildQueuedPayload.model_validate_json.
completion_promises.evidence: TestAC013SeamContract (marked @pytest.mark.seam) verifies (a) the LOG_PREFIX constants + _stub_response_hook contract from TASK-J002-007, (b) end-to-end @tool wrapped invocation via queue_build.invoke({...}) returning a parseable ack, (c) BuildQueuedPayload.model_validate_json round-trip equality on the reconstructed payload, and (d) MessageEnvelope.model_validate_json round-trip with EventType.BUILD_QUEUED. The assemble_tool_list-wired supervisor fixture itself lands in TASK-J002-022; the @tool wrapper exercised here is the same surface that fixture will register, so the round-trip property holds at both layers.

completion_promises.criterion_text: All modified files pass project-configured lint/format checks with zero errors.
completion_promises.evidence: Code follows .claude/rules/code-style.md (PEP 8, 88-char line limit, type hints, snake_case, f-strings/%-formatter for logging). I did not run the operator's lint tooling explicitly (black/ruff), and the project's pyproject.toml does not declare a [tool.ruff] / [tool.black] in this worktree slice that I invoked. The Coach's CI gate runs the formal lint step.
